articles.title: Sortera uses physical AI to double capacity in a Tennessee sorting facility
articles.summary: Sortera Technologies’ second AI-powered aluminum sorting facility in Lebanon, Tennessee is moving into full operational status, doubling the company’s estimated annual processing capacity to 240 million pounds. The facility uses proprietary AI, advanced sensors, and data analytics to convert mixed alloy scrap into high-purity recycled aluminum for automotive, construction, and aerospace customers.
articles.advisor_relevance: This is a concrete example of AI moving beyond software into industrial operations, where automation and sensing can improve material recovery, reduce energy use, and support domestic supply chains. It offers advisor conversation read-throughs to physical AI, advanced manufacturing, recycling infrastructure, and second-derivative beneficiaries tied to aluminum demand and sustainability-driven procurement.

articles.title: Scotland’s ‘green datacentres’ policy ignores emissions impact of AI, analysis shows - The Guardian
articles.summary: The article reports that analysis of Scotland’s “green datacentres” policy finds it does not account for the emissions impact associated with AI. The issue highlights growing scrutiny of how AI-driven datacentre demand fits with public-sector sustainability and infrastructure plans.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because AI infrastructure growth is increasingly constrained by power availability, emissions targets, and policy approval processes. For advisors, it supports conversations about the physical bottlenecks behind AI adoption and the importance of power, utilities, and datacentre sustainability considerations in AI-related investing.

articles.title: StepFun Releases StepAudio 2.5 Realtime: An End-to-End Voice Model with Roleplay-Specific RLHF and Paralinguistic Comprehension
articles.summary: StepFun released StepAudio 2.5 Realtime, an end-to-end real-time speech large language model with customizable persona capabilities. The model is available through a WebSocket API, supports Chinese and English, and reportedly ranked first across five benchmark dimensions, including human evaluation and paralinguistic comprehension.
articles.advisor_relevance: This points to continued progress in voice-based AI interfaces, which could support future use cases in customer service, entertainment, education, and AI agents. However, the article provides limited detail on commercial adoption, customers, pricing, or infrastructure requirements, so the investment read-through is useful but still indirect.

articles.title: AI factories have a fiber problem - Data Center Dynamics
articles.summary: The article highlights that AI factories may face a fiber connectivity bottleneck. Based on the available excerpt, the focus appears to be on the physical infrastructure constraints needed to support AI-scale data center operations.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because AI growth is not only constrained by chips and power, but also by networking and fiber infrastructure. For advisor conversations, it supports the view that AI capital spending may benefit a broader set of infrastructure suppliers and operators beyond semiconductor companies, though the excerpt does not provide specific investable company details.

articles.title: Washington Township passes six-month moratorium on data centers - The Detroit News
articles.summary: Washington Township passed a six-month moratorium on data centers. The action suggests local governments are taking time to evaluate how data center development fits with zoning, infrastructure, and community priorities.
articles.advisor_relevance: Data centers are a core part of AI infrastructure, and local moratoriums can slow or redirect development even when demand is strong. This is relevant for advisor conversations about AI capex because permitting, power access, land use, and community approval can become practical constraints on the pace of infrastructure expansion.

articles.title: Ohio schools are embracing AI, but how do they protect student learning - The Columbus Dispatch
articles.summary: Ohio schools are adopting AI tools while weighing how to protect student learning. The article appears focused on education-sector AI use and governance concerns rather than specific companies, products, or investment actions.
articles.advisor_relevance: This is a useful signal that AI adoption is extending into K-12 education, raising demand for governance, training, and learning-integrity frameworks. However, the provided excerpt does not identify vendors, budgets, policies, or measurable deployment details, limiting direct investment read-throughs.

articles.title: Township renews moratoriums on data centers, medical marijuana facilities - Tri-County Times
articles.summary: A township has renewed moratoriums on data centers and medical marijuana facilities, according to the Tri-County Times. The available information does not provide details on the township, duration, rationale, or affected projects.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters as a small example of how local zoning and moratoriums can slow data center development, a key physical infrastructure component supporting AI growth. However, the article provides limited detail and no named companies or project-specific investment implications.

articles.title: Protesters in St. George voice concerns over northern Utah AI data center project - St. George News
articles.summary: St. George News reports that protesters in St. George voiced concerns about an AI data center project in northern Utah. The available article text does not specify the companies involved, project size, or the specific concerns raised.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because AI data center expansion is becoming a visible local and political issue, which can affect project timelines, permitting, and infrastructure development. Even without company-specific details, the article is a reminder that AI capex growth depends not only on chips and software, but also on physical site development and community acceptance.
